title: 'Not how you build a digital mind': How reasoning failures are preventing AI models from achieving human-level intelligence
summary: Existing LLM architecture may not support the problem-solving capabilities needed to underpin human-level AI, the authors of a new study argue.
author: Owen Hughes

title: Evaluating the ethics of autonomous systems
summary: MIT researchers developed a testing framework that pinpoints situations where AI decision-support systems are not treating people and communities fairly.
author: Adam Zewe | MIT News

title: SOMATIC – bathroom cleaning robots for office buildings
summary: Why it matters: Discover how SOMATIC bathroom cleaning robots use VR training and AI to autonomously clean commercial restrooms in office buildings, saving up to 50% on janitorial costs.
author: Sanksshep Mahendra

title: Did Google's TurboQuant Actually Solve AI Memory Crunch?
summary: Google’s TurboQuant promises 6x KV-cache compression. Here’s what it means for AI memory, HBM demand, and the broader memory crunch.
author: Vinit Mehta

title: Who’s Doing What on Your Network? by Mark Wojtasiak
summary: Can you confidently answer who is doing what on your network? Learn why visibility into user activity is key to security, risk, and compliance.

title: #claude Chat vs. Cowork: Biggest 3 differences
summary:
#ClaudeCowork is the next level up from #Claude Chat. Here are the 3 biggest shifts in how you work.

Shift 1: Claude Chat limits you to 20 files per conversation and 30MB per file, since everything uploads to the cloud. Claude Cowork reads directly from your computer, so there's no file limit and no size cap.

Shift 2: Claude Chat has a much smaller context window, meaning it starts compacting your conversation and losing important details way sooner than Claude Cowork.

Shift 3: Claude Chat gives you answers in a chat window, so you still have to do something with it yourself. Claude Cowork delivers ready-to-use files directly in your folder. I gave it 100+ receipts and got back a complete expense report in Excel, even flagging rows for me to review.
author: Jeff Su
